seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: input: relax the seed's generic balanced max-cut into a planted-bipartition thermal-coupling graph where heavy cross-side couplings and light intra-side noise are mixed and vertex labels are randomly permuted, so the equal-capacity max-bisection is NP-hard and random/greedy/Kernighan-Lin strategies genuinely diverge above a moderate index-split baseline.
title: Twin-Loop Cooling: Balanced Thermal Zone Split
strategies: reference index split: racks 1..n/2 on blue, rest on red (the calibration baseline)
greedy assignment: process racks by descending incident coupling weight, place each on the capacity-remaining loop that maximizes marginal split strength
Kernighan-Lin swap local search: from a balanced start, repeatedly apply the best cut-improving swap of one blue rack with one red rack until no improvement
randomized multi-restart local search: run KL swaps from many random balanced starts and keep the best-scoring balanced partition found
